headline: GE Vernova Q1 Earnings Blow Past Expectations, Stock Jumps 14%
summary: GEV reported EPS of $17.44 and sales of $9.3B, smashing estimates. Raised full-year revenue forecast to $44.5-45.5B. Secured $2.4B in datacenter equipment orders in Q1 alone — more than all of 2025. Stock up 246% over past year.

headline: TSMC Q1 Revenue Surges 41% YoY to $35.9B, Unveils A13 and N2U Chip Tech
summary: TSMC reported Q1 2026 revenue growth of 41% YoY with gross margin of 66%. Advanced nodes (7nm and below) represent 74% of wafer revenue, 3nm at 25%. Unveiled new A13 and N2U manufacturing processes for next-gen AI chips. Stock rose 5%.

headline: FERC Commits to June 2026 Action on Large Load Interconnection Rules
summary: On April 16, FERC announced it will finalize reforms governing how large loads (especially data centers) connect to the interstate transmission grid by end of June 2026. Staff reviewed 3,500+ pages of public comments. Already approved PJM co-location rules and SPP HILL initiative.

headline: Vertiv Q1 Sales Jump 30% to $2.65B, Beats on EPS but Guidance Disappoints
summary: Net sales rose 30% to $2.65B with adj. EPS of $1.17 vs $1.00 estimate. However, Q2 guidance of $1.37-1.43 EPS came in below $1.43 consensus. Stock dipped 2.3% despite strong underlying demand from hyperscaler buildouts.

headline: CoreWeave Revenue Backlog Hits $87.8B After $21B Meta Deal Expansion
summary: Meta signed up for additional $21B in AI processing capacity through Dec 2032, bringing CoreWeave backlog to $87.8B. Meta now represents 40.1% of backlog. CoreWeave projecting $30-35B capex for full 2026, with 43 data centers and ~600K GPUs.

headline: Jensen Huang: Manufacturing Bottlenecks Are a 2-3 Year Problem
summary: NVIDIA CEO warned that GPU manufacturing constraints will persist for 2-3 years. Despite this, Huang sees a $1 trillion AI chip sales opportunity in 2026-2027 alone. Supply constraints benefit pricing power but may slow datacenter buildout timelines.

headline: Nuclear SMR Pipeline for Datacenters Grows to 45 GW from 25 GW in 2024
summary: Conditional offtake agreements between DC operators and SMR projects have nearly doubled to 45 GW. NRC Part 53 framework expected by early 2027. TerraPower construction permit expected after mid-2026. Meta has 20-year nuclear purchase agreements with three plants.

headline: Hyperscaler CapEx Projections Surge to $660-750B for 2026, Nearly Doubling 2025
summary: Amazon leads at $200B, followed by Alphabet $175-185B, Meta $115-135B, Microsoft ~$120B, Oracle $50B. Capex-to-sales ratios reaching extreme levels: Oracle 86%, Meta 54%, Microsoft 47%. Sector may need $1.5T in new debt over coming years.

headline: Stifel Upgrades Texas Instruments to Buy, Raises Target to $250
summary: Stifel upgraded TXN from Hold to Buy with PT of $250 (from $215), expecting FCF inflection after six years of elevated capex. TXN benefits from growing analog chip demand in datacenter power management and cooling systems.

headline: US Datacenter Delays and Cancellations Creating Winners in Power Infrastructure
summary: Massive delays in US datacenter buildouts due to grid constraints are benefiting companies that provide behind-the-meter power solutions, distributed generation, and grid equipment. Solaris Energy filed $226M shelf registration for BTM expansion.

headline: Bloom Energy Soars 31% on Oracle 2.8 GW Fuel Cell Deal for AI Datacenters
summary: Oracle agreed to procure up to 2.8 GW of Bloom Energy fuel cell systems under a master services agreement, with 1.2 GW already contracted for deployment in 2026-2027. Bloom delivered a system to Oracle in just 55 days last year vs 90-day target. BE stock surged 31%. Deal underscores behind-the-meter fuel cells as fastest path to datacenter power.

headline: IEA: Data Center Electricity to Hit 1,000 TWh in 2026, AI-Focused DCs to Triple by 2030
summary: IEA reports DC electricity surged 17% in 2025 and will reach ~1,000 TWh in 2026 (equivalent to Japan). AI-focused DC power to triple by 2030. Tech sector signed 40% of all corporate renewable PPAs in 2025. SMR pipeline for DCs grew from 25 GW to 45 GW.

headline: SMCI Craters 27% on Indictment; Dell Gains AI Server Share with $43B Backlog
summary: Federal prosecutors indicted Super Micro co-founder on charges of illegally smuggling Nvidia AI servers to China. SMCI plunged 27%. Enterprise customers shifting to Dell, which reported $8.95B in AI server revenue in Q4 FY26 (+342% YoY) and $43B backlog. Dell stock +5%.

headline: White House Space Nuclear Mandate Sparks Rally in Oklo, NuScale, Nano Nuclear
summary: White House mandated space nuclear deployment by 2028 and lunar reactors by 2030, sparking sector-wide rally. NuScale +11% to $12.65, Oklo +6% to $67.88. Bank of America sees nuclear renaissance as $10T opportunity, but meaningful SMR capacity not expected until 2035.

headline: Constellation Energy Down 25% from Highs; Morgan Stanley Resumes at Overweight, $385 PT
summary: CEG trading near $301, down 25% from 52-week high after $16.4B Calpine acquisition in Jan 2026 added nat gas and geothermal assets. Morgan Stanley resumed coverage at Overweight with $385 target (27% upside). Vistra has nuclear-to-datacenter deals with AWS and Meta.

headline: Eaton Creates Dedicated Data Center Segment After $9.5B Boyd Acquisition
summary: Eaton formally created a dedicated Data Center segment following its $9.5B Boyd thermal acquisition, focusing on integrated power and liquid cooling for AI DCs. Committed $30M+ to expand medium-voltage switchgear production in Nebraska. Stock at $425, up 55% in 1Y. Q1 earnings May 5.

headline: Data Centers Now Account for 50% of All New US Electricity Demand
summary: Fortune reports that data centers now represent half of all new electricity consumption in the US, even as public opinion on AI begins to sour. ITIF counters with four reasons DCs won't overwhelm the grid, citing efficiency gains and distributed generation.

headline: Dell Technologies AI Server Revenue Hits $8.95B in Q4 FY26 (+342% YoY)
summary: Dell booked $64B in AI orders in FY26 and entered FY27 with $43B backlog. Forward P/E of 13.5x vs 17% annualized earnings growth makes Dell one of the most undervalued AI infrastructure plays. Gaining share from SMCI amid governance concerns.
